framework.name: TrustStack Evaluation Standard
framework.description: A weighted, evidence-first evaluation standard for TrustStack answers with claim support, contradiction scanning, calibration diagnostics, and standardized report export.

dimensions.label: Retrieval relevance
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the retrieved evidence is strongly aligned with the question instead of relying on one weak match.

dimensions.label: Evidence sufficiency
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer is backed by enough non-duplicative support across the corpus.

dimensions.label: Citation traceability
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether citations point back to the retrieved evidence and cover the answer transparently.

dimensions.label: Claim support
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer's individual claims are explicitly supported by the retrieved evidence.

dimensions.label: Contradiction risk
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer appears to conflict with the retrieved evidence.

dimensions.label: Completeness
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer addresses the major terms and demands of the question.

dimensions.label: Honesty and abstention
dimensions.purpose: Rewards the model for acknowledging uncertainty instead of overstating support.

dimensions.label: Answer discipline
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer stays proportional to the evidence without drifting into unsupported detail.

dimensions.label: Safety and operational risk
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the answer contains procedural or high-stakes guidance that needs review.

dimensions.label: Calibration and consistency
dimensions.purpose: Measures whether the overall score and explanation are calibrated to the evidence quality and detected weaknesses.

recommended_actions: Compare task-level benchmark accuracy to TrustStack score to identify where groundedness and task success diverge.
Inspect datasets with weak citation alignment before claiming strong evidence traceability on public benchmarks.
Use FEVER and SciFact to pressure contradiction handling, and HotpotQA to pressure multi-hop evidence synthesis.

cases.question: Claim: 0-dimensional biomaterials show inductive properties.. Based only on the evidence, is this claim supported, contradicted, or not enough information? Respond with exactly one label: supported, contradicted, or not enough information.

Claim: 1,000 genomes project enables mapping of genetic sequence variation consisting of rare variants with larger penetrance effects than common variants.. Based only on the evidence, is this claim supported, contradicted, or not enough information? Respond with exactly one label: supported, contradicted, or not enough information.

Claim: 1/2000 in UK have abnormal PrP positivity.. Based only on the evidence, is this claim supported, contradicted, or not enough information? Respond with exactly one label: supported, contradicted, or not enough information.

Claim: 5% of perinatal mortality is due to low birth weight.. Based only on the evidence, is this claim supported, contradicted, or not enough information? Respond with exactly one label: supported, contradicted, or not enough information.
Were Scott Derrickson and Ed Wood of the same nationality? Answer with the shortest grounded phrase possible. If the evidence is insufficient, answer: not enough information.
cases.predicted_answer: Much of his work is in the action and horror film genres, with films like "Dawn of the Dead, 300, Sucker Punch," and "John Wick."  He has collaborated with directors like Zack Snyder, Rob Zombie, Neil Marshall, William Friedkin, Scott Derrickson, and James Gunn. Woodson and its accompanying Woodson Lake and Wood Hollow are the namesake for Ed Wood Sr., a prominent plantation owner, trader, and businessman at the turn of the 20th century. With Gunn, he has scored every one of the director's films; including "Guardians of the Galaxy", which became one of the highest grossing domestic movies of 2014, and its 2017 sequel.

cases.question: What government position was held by the woman who portrayed Corliss Archer in the film Kiss and Tell? Answer with the shortest grounded phrase possible. If the evidence is insufficient, answer: not enough information.
cases.predicted_answer: Kiss and Tell is a 1945 American comedy film starring then 17-year-old Shirley Temple as Corliss Archer. The position is known as the "karnam" in Andhra Pradesh, "patnaik" in Orissa or "adhikari" in Tamil Nadu, while it is commonly known as the "talati" in Karnataka, Gujarat and Maharashtra. The position was known as the "kulkarni" in Northern Karnataka and Maharashtra.

cases.question: What science fantasy young adult series, told in first person, has a set of companion books narrating the stories of enslaved worlds and alien species? Answer with the shortest grounded phrase possible. If the evidence is insufficient, answer: not enough information.
cases.predicted_answer: Animorphs is a science fantasy series of young adult books written by Katherine Applegate and her husband Michael Grant, writing together under the name K. It is told in first person, with all six main characters taking turns narrating the books through their own perspectives. Her first three books, "The Seer And The Sword", "The Healer's Keep" and "The Light Of The Oracle" are companion books to one another.

cases.question: Are the Laleli Mosque and Esma Sultan Mansion located in the same neighborhood? Answer with the shortest grounded phrase possible. If the evidence is insufficient, answer: not enough information.
cases.predicted_answer: A popular tourist site, the Sultan Ahmed Mosque continues to function as a mosque today; men still kneel in prayer on the mosque's lush red carpet after the call to prayer. Hand-painted blue tiles adorn the mosque’s interior walls, and at night the mosque is bathed in blue as lights frame the mosque’s five main domes, six minarets and eight secondary domes. The Sultan Ahmed Mosque or Sultan Ahmet Mosque (Turkish: "Sultan Ahmet Camii" ) is a historic mosque located in Istanbul, Turkey.

cases.question: The director of the romantic comedy "Big Stone Gap" is based in what New York city? Answer with the shortest grounded phrase possible. If the evidence is insufficient, answer: not enough information.
cases.predicted_answer: In 1926 the Society was formalized and incorporated under the laws of the State of New York. Only the ornate waiting room for the recently discontinued ferry boats to 23rd Street in New York City. The main plot was taken from the Russian romantic comedy "The Irony of Fate" (1976).
